_status: AMENDED to v2 (rule-only extractor). Re-freeze as prereg-legal-reversal-v2. No data unblinded between v1 and v2 (no S-vs-reversal examined), so the amendment is clean.
_amendment: v1 (prereg-legal-reversal-v1, frozen) specified an LLM+SRL extractor. v2 changes the extractor to RULE-ONLY (spaCy-SRL + LaBSE, no LLM). Rationale: (1) removes LLM stochasticity -> deterministic, reproducible pipeline; (2) removes the LLM-circularity vulnerability (an LLM trained on the behavioral/legal literature could reflect it rather than the geometry); (3) CPU-only, no LLM endpoint dependency. Validated: the rule extractor compiles real district opinions to the 9-dim MoralTensorV3 with no LLM. Nothing else in the frozen prediction changes. Amended before any label linkage / S computation was examined.

corpus.source: CourtListener bulk 2026-06-30 dump, loaded to the Atlas Postgres replica (public-domain, CC Public Domain Mark; reproducible). 10.8M opinions, 77.5M citation edges, 71.7M dockets, 973K originating-court-info, judges.
corpus.westlaw_role: label-validation-and-taxonomy-reference-only (no bulk extraction; ToS + TR v. ROSS 2025)
corpus.court_set: UNIT = U.S. District Court merits opinions (search_court.jurisdiction='FD'); REVIEWER = the U.S. Court of Appeals opinion (jurisdiction='F') linked via search_docket.originating_court_information_id -> search_originatingcourtinformation.docket_number (authoritative appeal lineage). 248,364 circuit appeals carry lower-court lineage (feasibility confirmed).
corpus.year_range: district decisions filed 1990-01-01 .. 2015-12-31 (appellate history matured by the 2026 dump)
corpus.unit_dedup: one row per reviewed district merits opinion; exclude non-merits dispositions per label.excluded

splits.rule: temporal by district-decision filing date; train < t0, validate [t0,t1), test >= t1; no unit in two splits; the low-rank manifold and legal-BERT baseline are fit on train only

label.positive: reversed OR vacated in direct appellate history
label.source: CONSTRUCTED (not a field): full-text NLP + citation-graph linkage
construction_pipeline: 1. get full opinion text (CL token or bulk)
2. link each lower decision to the appellate opinion reviewing it (citation graph + docket + sibling_ids)
3. NLP-parse the appellate opinion's holding for reverse/vacate vs affirm (label y)
4. NLP-extract the GROUND of reversal and map to the deontic gates (gate-aligned target)
5. Westlaw KeyCite gold-standard validation on the sample below
label.construction_is_a_project: steps 2-4 are substantial, noisy NLP+graph engineering with their own error; label quality must be measured, not assumed

stratify_by_ground.rationale: the compiler measures MORAL structure; reversal tracks LEGAL error. They align for rights/procedure grounds, not for precedential/statutory ones. Stratify (and pre-commit the primary target = gate-relevant reversals) so the measured pathology matches the label.
stratify_by_ground.primary_target: reversals on gate-aligned grounds (sharper, more uniquely-geometric)

compiler.pipeline: erisml-compiler (MoralGraph -> MoralTensorV3 + projections); IR schema erisml_compiler_ir_v0.3; Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20659432
compiler.NOT_aesthetics_compiler: the aesthetics-compiler emits visual dims (color/contrast/closure) and is the WRONG tool; the ErisML compiler emits normative structure
compiler.tensor: MoralTensorV3; rank-2 (k,n) primary [k=moral dimension, n=stakeholder], rank-1 (k,) global fallback. Only ranks 1-2 are wired (higher ranks raise NotImplementedError -- honest contract).
compiler.coordinate_match: these k-dimensions are ~the GDT 9-D decision-manifold coordinates (rights/fairness/autonomy/privacy/legitimacy/epistemic/virtue/social); the compiler is in effect a concrete GDT encoder -- the low-rank pillar and pathology score live in the same space as the rest of the program
compiler.extractor: v2: RULE-ONLY -- MoralGraph built by spaCy-SRL + LaBSE, NO LLM (erisml tier=rules, extractor=rule). Deterministic; validated on real district opinions -> 9-dim MoralTensorV3. Pinned as part of the frozen pipeline (erisml-compiler @ the commit installed on Atlas).
compiler.input_text: court's own opinion, redacted per label.leakage_redaction

pathology_score_S.space: the MoralTensorV3 (GDT-coordinate) representation of each opinion
pathology_score_S.k_rule: smallest k with marginal explained variance < 0.01 (fit on train only, then frozen)
pathology_score_S.standardization: z-score each component on the TRAIN split, then S = 0.4*z(off_manifold) + 0.3*z(curvature) + 0.3*z(gate_failures); apply the train transforms to val/test

H1_reversal.metric: OR per SD of S, selection-corrected

H2_unique.metric: held-out ΔAUC of S over legal-BERT baseline

H3_structure.metric: monotonicity of reversal in off-manifold residual + boundary elevation

selection_control.preferred: random-trial-judge-assignment IV (examiner design) if court set supports it
selection_control.fallback: Heckman two-stage (stage1 P(appeal), stage2 P(reverse|appeal) with inverse-Mills)
selection_control.reporting: raw AND selection-corrected; headline = corrected; H1 requires sign/threshold stable across both

multiplicity: Holm across the 5 confirmatory rows; all else exploratory
min_test_n_note: do not run confirmatory tests below 20,000 reviewed district decisions in the test window; 248,364 circuit appeals carry lineage corpus-wide, so the 1990-2015 district->circuit test split is expected to clear this comfortably. If it does not, expand the window before (not after) unblinding.
label_construction_engine: cl_labels_sql.py on the Atlas replica: authoritative docket-lineage linkage (originating_court_information) + reverse-citation graph; disposition/ground via NLP on the reviewing circuit opinion; Westlaw KeyCite validation on the 300-sample.
compute: Stage 4 (ErisML compile -> MoralTensor -> S) is embarrassingly parallel per opinion; plan is to shard ~100-300k district opinions across NRP GPU pods reusing the llm-panel polite/preemption-tolerant harness. Post-freeze.
tier_gate: Tier-A success REQUIRES H1 AND H2 AND H3. H1-only-with-H2-fail => reported as Tier B (re-description). Committed, not reframable post hoc.

caveats.moral_vs_legal: the compiler encodes MORAL structure (GDT coordinates), not the full legal apparatus (precedent, statutory interpretation, procedure). Reversals driven by purely legal-technical error are invisible to it -- hence the gate-aligned primary target. A null on all-grounds reversal with a hit on gate-aligned reversal is the expected, honest shape.
caveats.extractor: v2: the MoralGraph is RULE/SRL-extracted (no LLM), so it is deterministic and free of LLM-absorbed-literature circularity. It still reflects the SRL extractor's reading (a source of noise), so the extractor + erisml-compiler version are pinned as part of the frozen pipeline.
caveats.single_jurisdiction: one court set / window; positive result is not universal (cross-court replication is future work).
